channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: More 'city-killer' asteroids apparent, but fret not: astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: Seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Matias Delacroix And Juan Zamorano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis suffering from early stage kidney failure, though condition remains 'under control': Vatican
item.description: Vatican officials announced on Sunday that Pope Francis is currently suffering from mild renal insufficiency, also known as kidney failure, amid his treatment for bilateral pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU leaders will hold an emergency summit on Ukraine and European security
item.description: The leaders of the 27 European Union countries will hold an emergency summit on March 6 about next steps for Ukraine and Europe’s security.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi lands safely in Rome after security concern
item.description: American Airlines says one of its flights from New York to the Indian capital, New Delhi, landed safely in Rome on Sunday afternoon after it was diverted due to a security concern, which later proved to be “non-credible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis remains in 'critical condition' with signs of early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis remains hospitalized in "critical condition" with pneumonia, but the Vatican says he rested well overnight.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shares written message while continuing hospitalization
item.description: Pope Francis purportedly shared a written message from his hospital bed while remaining in critical condition and continuing treatment in Rome.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French aircraft carrier stages combat drills with Filipinos in disputed sea and visits Philippines
item.description: ABOARD THE CHARLES DE GAULLE, Philippines (AP) — France’s nuclear-powered aircraft carrier and accompanying warships were in the Philippines on Sunday after holding combat drills with Filipino forces in the disputed South China Sea in a show of firepower that would likely antagonize China.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: An Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after the Taliban lifted their suspension over alleged cooperation with an overseas TV channel. Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power amid the chaotic withdrawal of U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis had peaceful night's rest at hospital following respiratory crisis, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, rested during a peaceful ninth night at Rome's Gemelli Hospital after a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, the Vatican said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of people have packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets for the funeral of Hezbollah’s leader, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike on a southern suburb of the capital.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue And Abby Sewell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis' health 'looking very serious': Vatican reporter
item.description: When journalist Colm Flynn last saw Pope Francis, the Holy Father was “in good spirits” but visibly “struggling to catch his breath.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Isolated in 'harsh conditions:' Deportee from US details legal limbo in Panama camp near Darien Gap
item.description: CARTI, Panama (AP) — A deportee from the United States detained in a camp in rural Panama, among a hundred who refused to return to their countries, described on Saturday waiting in limbo under “harsh conditions” and cut off from access to legal council and other rights.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a prolonged asthmatic respiratory crisis while being treated for pneumonia and a complex lung infection, the Vatican said. The 88-year-old pope, who remains conscious, received “high flows” of oxygen to help him breathe.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered an asthmatic respiratory crisis that required oxygen, the Associated Press reported. The 88-year-old pope has been hospitalized for over a week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hungarians will decide whether Ukraine can join the European Union, Orbán says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Hungary will be the one to decide whether Ukraine is able to achieve its hopes of joining the European Union in the future, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán said Saturday in an escalation of his adversarial posture toward Hungary's war-ravaged neighbor.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 70 Christians beheaded in African country by ISIS-aligned militants, groups say; world mostly silent
item.description: Reports indicate that 70 Christians were beheaded by Islamic militants in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The group that killed the faithful is an affiliate of the Islamic state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews, investigators say
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The suspect in a stabbing attack at Berlin's Holocaust Memorial that seriously injured a Spanish tourist is a Syrian refugee who apparently wanted to kill Jews, investigators said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis isn't out of danger but his condition isn't life-threatening, medical team says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ complex respiratory infection isn't life-threatening but he's not out of danger, his medical team said Friday, as the 88-year-old pontiff marked his first week in the hospital battling pneumonia in both lungs — along with bacterial, viral and fungal infections on top of his
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An encroaching desert threatens to swallow Mauritania’s homes and history
item.description: CHINGUETTI, Mauritania (AP) — For centuries, poets, scholars and theologians have flocked to Chinguetti, a trans-Saharan trading post home to more than a dozen libraries containing thousands of manuscripts. But it now stands on the brink of oblivion.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Zelensky 'has no cards,' 'shouldn't be at meetings' with Russia
item.description: President Trump lashed out at Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday, saying he did not think it was important for Zelensky to attend negotiations about the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 20 Mexican cities, 5 in US among 50 most dangerous in the world
item.description: At the top of the list is Haiti's Port-au-Prince with 139 homicides per 100,000 residents, followed by five cities in Mexico: Colima, Manzanillo, Acapulco, Tijuana and Ciudad Obregón.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Rwanda expresses solidarity with military general sanctioned by the US over violence in Congo
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Rwanda's government on Friday asserted solidarity with a top official sanctioned by the U.S. over violence in eastern Congo, where Rwanda-backed rebels have captured two major cities in an armed conflict that now threatens the Congolese government.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s attempts to denigrate Zelenskyy have led to a surge in Ukrainian unity
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Days before the third anniversary of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukrainians are as somber and tense as they were right before Moscow launched the war. Only now, they aren’t just worried about their longtime enemy.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military said Friday it had positively identified the remains of two young hostages but another body released by Hamas under a ceasefire deal was not the boys’ mother as the militant group had promised.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, JOSEF FEDERMAN AND WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis has bilateral pneumonia but remains in good spirits, the Vatican says
item.description: Nevertheless the pope is in good spirits and is grateful for the prayers for his recovery, Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
